desc: Conwy is one of the strongest short-break bases in North Wales if you want medieval walls and mountain access in the same trip: a heavyweight castle, a harbour that still feels compact and real, quick rail links, and easy onward hops into Eryri, Llandudno or Anglesey. It is compact enough to do without a car, but popular enough that sunny weekends and school holidays change the mood fast.

todo.name: Conwy Castle
todo.desc: Walk the battlements for estuary, mountain and walled-town views in one compact circuit.

todo.name: Town walls loop
todo.desc: Circle the medieval walls to understand Conwy's layout beyond the castle itself.

todo.name: Conwy harbour
todo.desc: Pause by the quay for mussels, boats and the Smallest House landmark.

todo.name: Eryri/Snowdonia side trip
todo.desc: Use Conwy as a coast base for mountain railways, lakes or lower-level walks when weather suits.

todo.name: Plas Mawr and old-town lanes
todo.desc: Add Britain's best-preserved Elizabethan town house and the backstreets around it for more than just castle views.

todo.name: Harbour and suspension bridge
todo.desc: Use the quay, estuary edge and Telford bridge for the best of Conwy's water-and-walls perspective.

wifi.note: Generally reliable in hotels and cafes inside the walls, but the station itself is basic and public coverage is patchier once you head out toward the coast or mountains.

prac.fltNote: Conwy has no airport and no destination-level LGW/LCY nonstop routing. Manchester and Liverpool are the practical air gateways, but rail often matters more than flying because Conwy station sits right by the town and onward North Wales links are straightforward. The main friction is not route choice so much as parking, old-town congestion and peak-day pressure on headline sights.
prac.visa: UK domestic destination for British citizens; Irish and other travellers should verify UK entry rules if applicable.
alerts: Castle tickets, harbour parking and town-centre circulation are busiest on school-holiday days and sunny weekends.
Mountain weather can differ sharply from the coast even when Conwy itself looks settled.
Narrow old-town streets are easier without a car.
Conwy station is useful but limited, so do not expect big-city facilities there.

sweet: May, June and September are the best balance for castle time, harbour walks and Eryri side trips without the thickest summer-weekend pressure.
